**Developing the Database Idea**

I'm now fully immersed in the project's core. The goal is to build a React single-page application focused on sacred frequencies. The database will encompass various frequency types, from Solfeggio to planetary and bodily organs. Pyramidal and megalithic frequencies are also in scope. I’m thinking about the most efficient data structure to support search, filtering, and detailed frequency displays.


**Adding New Data Dimensions**

I've expanded my focus to include detailed descriptions, usage insights, and evidence for each frequency type. The initial scope now incorporates brainwaves and synergies, aiming for a richer user experience with comprehensive context. This involves careful data modeling to ensure efficient search and retrieval across these diverse categories. The goal remains a "World-class" React app with Tailwind CSS for style.


**Outlining the UI Structure**

I've just been fleshing out the application's component architecture, envisioning the user interface now. Components like `FrequencyCard`, `Header`, and `Controls` are taking shape.  The data will be statically imported from a `frequencies.ts` file after I fully define the data types, which are my next priority. The UI is designed to be very visual, with a goal to potentially include glassmorphism and aurora effects.


**Finalizing Component Design**

I'm now refining the component architecture, focusing on data integration and UI specifics.  I've defined data structures and added `numericalHz` for sorting, and I am moving towards building out key elements:  a holographic header, a floating control panel, and a grid of `FrequencyCards` showcasing frequencies with glassmorphism. My next steps are in rendering the main app with the state and effects in place.


**Revising Design Details**

I'm now zeroing in on the finer points. I've chosen a font (Inter) and will use it in various weights. I'm focusing on color accents to mirror the frequency categories and ensuring that I'm implementing sorting logic and icons properly (using Lucide). Performance is not a worry with a static list, and I'm currently adding the user's Spanish content to the TS file, carefully keeping the text in Spanish.


**Reviewing and Refining Implementation**

I've been meticulously reviewing my code and design choices.  Optimized filtering logic is in place, and the glassmorphism effects are working beautifully. My primary focus now is finalizing the cosmic aurora background using CSS animations. I've also incorporated the user's "Arpeggios" concept within the "Synergy" category. This final polish involves meticulous checks of import paths and strict mode compliance.


**Adjusting Data to Scope**

I'm now implementing the 432Hz tuning notes the user requested. Although the existing frequencies align with the provided data, I'll ensure the UI remains flexible. I will make sure the UI accommodates future expansion of the frequency types, even if the "Sacred Frequencies" are all included. I'm focusing on the data's integrity and adherence to the user's HTML example.

payload.text: Implementaremos funciones, opciones y configuraciones para la reproducción de frecuencias. Se creará una pestaña de reproducción con opciones de visualización de las ondas individuales y de la onda resultante combinada. Cada onda podrá configurarse de forma ilimitada en cuanto a tipo de onda, frecuencia, filtros y volumen, así como la opción de reproducción en el canal derecho, izquierdo o ambos. Se incluirá la posibilidad de modificar la frecuencia mediante escritura directa o deslizamiento. Se calculará la onda binaural resultante al combinar todas las frecuencias, si es el caso. Se permitirá añadir frecuencias, con la opción de combinar frecuencias o reproducirlas por separado. Adicionalmente, se incorporará una pestaña con un reproductor visible en todas las secciones, que mostrará un resumen de las frecuencias con funciones básicas como detener o reproducir, la visualización de la onda resultante y la indicación de las frecuencias que se están reproduciendo actualmente. Haz también una opción para añadir cualquier frecuencia desde cada opción de la lista de frecuencias de cada pestaña, y que al añadirla se seleccionen sus configuraciones y características de la onda.

**Analyzing the Goal**

I've been meticulously analyzing the request. The core is adding audio playback, frequency synthesis, and visualization to the existing system. I'm breaking down these core components to see how they'll mesh.


**Outlining the Core Features**

I've been making major progress in outlining the app's fundamental architecture. The "Playback" tab is becoming the hub, with the oscillator configuration details nailed down. I've got wave type, frequency, volume, and pan controls set, and am thinking about filters. The app will feature individual and combined wave visualization, and include binaural beat detection logic. A global player is also in the works for consistent control.


**Designing the Controls**

I'm now focusing on the user interface and the audio engine integration. Basic controls like Play/Stop and an active frequencies summary are becoming concrete. I've designed an "Add to Player" button to link existing frequency cards to the oscillator. The design maintains the core sci-fi aesthetic.


**Refining the Structure**

I'm currently revising the app's structure to match the feature list. I've broken it down to specific components: `App.tsx` (state management and routing), `AudioEngine.ts` (Web Audio API), `Generator.tsx` (the frequency mixer), `OscillatorRow.tsx` (individual oscillator controls), `GlobalPlayer.tsx` (the global player), `Visualizer.tsx` (waveform visualization), and an updated `FrequencyCard.tsx`. I'm starting to think about data types for the `OscillatorConfig`.


**Building the UI Elements**

I'm now fully immersed in developing the user interface components. I've begun crafting the `Generator` component, which will be the central hub for adding and managing oscillators, complete with a master visualizer. I'm also working on the `OscillatorRow` component, the individual frequency controls, including small, optional wave visualizers. I've started the global player, and have made key updates to the frequency card to include an "Add" button and a callback. Finally, I'm working to integrate a main library view and generator view switch.


**Incorporating Audio Analysis**

I'm now fully immersed in developing the user interface components. I've begun crafting the `Generator` component, which will be the central hub for adding and managing oscillators, complete with a master visualizer. I'm also working on the `OscillatorRow` component, the individual frequency controls, including small, optional wave visualizers. I've started the global player, and have made key updates to the frequency card to include an "Add" button and a callback. Finally, I'm working to integrate a main library view and generator view switch.  Now, I'm focusing on incorporating the analyser node for visual feedback. Also, I'm integrating the binaural logic; the app will allow distinct L/R frequencies.


**Integrating Visual Feedback**

I'm now integrating the visualizer, which will naturally display interference patterns from overlapping waves. I'm also adding "deslizamiento" (slider) controls using range input elements.  I'm now focusing on ensuring that the core Web Audio API logic is integrated seamlessly within the UI.


**Optimizing the Visualization**

I'm now revising my visualization plan, realizing I need multiple AnalyserNodes. I'll include a master analyser for the combined output and individual analysers for each oscillator for their unique waveforms.  I've decided to prioritize the master visualizer, and include the individual visualisation, as requested. I'll use SVGs for performance, or a simplified canvas, per oscillator.

Aquí tienes una actualización completa de la aplicación que integra un potente **Sintetizador de Frecuencias Holográfico**.

Esta actualización incluye:

1.  **Nuevo Motor de Audio (`useAudio`)**: Gestiona múltiples osciladores, mezcla, paneo (L/R) y visualización en tiempo real.
2.  **Pestaña "Generador"**: Una consola de mezcla donde puedes añadir osciladores ilimitados, configurar tipo de onda (Senoidal, Cuadrada, etc.), volumen, paneo y frecuencia con sliders y texto.
3.  **Visualizadores**:
    *   **Master**: Visualización en tiempo real de la onda resultante (donde se pueden ver los batidos binaurales visualmente).
    *   **Individuales**: Cada canal tiene su propio pequeño osciloscopio.
4.  **Reproductor Global**: Una barra persistente en la parte inferior para controlar la reproducción general desde cualquier sección.
5.  **Integración en la Biblioteca**: Ahora cada tarjeta en la lista de frecuencias tiene un botón **"Añadir al Player"** que carga esa frecuencia específica en el generador automáticamente.

También las opciones de combinar frecuencia o reproducir aparte; en ese caso se debe visualizar esa onda también en la misma gráfica de la resonancia maestra con algún color diferente editable.

**Examining the Core Logic**

I've been drilling into the core logic, dissecting the suggested updates for the "Generator" and "Audio Engine." The focus now is pinpointing how to effectively calculate and display binaural beat frequencies, a new capability. This involves mapping the existing architecture to integrate and enhance.


**Analyzing Feature Implementation**

I'm now deep-diving into the implementation details for the binaural beat calculation and spatial audio controls. Regarding binaural beats, the detection logic seems straightforward for dominant oscillators, easily integrating into the existing structure. For the Spatial Audio expansion, the Web Audio API's `PannerNode` provides the necessary tools for 3D panning. I'm focusing on user interface aspects and efficient integration, specifically ensuring the visualization reflects the spatial parameters accurately.


**Defining 3D Spatial Audio**

I'm now focusing on 3D spatialization. The `PannerNode` is key, with `positionX`, `positionY`, and `positionZ` properties crucial for simulating spatial audio, requiring a defined listener position. The visualization update requires that the `Visualizer` handle an array of AnalyserNodes or data sources, so it can represent each sound source separately. The idea is to visualize oscillator's analyser as overlays, rather than combined sounds. I have started a step-by-step implementation plan including updates to `types.ts`, `OscillatorState`, and related properties.


**Integrating Spatial Controls**

I'm now implementing the 3D controls and visualization enhancements. I've updated `useAudio.ts` to manage the listener's position and utilize `PannerNode` for spatial positioning, including a new `calculateBinauralBeats()` function. Next, I'll rework the Visualizer to accept and draw multiple analyser nodes, representing each oscillator individually. OscillatorControls will be upgraded with X, Y, Z sliders, a "Visualize Separately" toggle, and color picker. Finally, I'll pass specific analysers to the master visualizer.


**Enhancing Binaural Beat Display**

I'm now focusing on displaying the calculated binaural beat frequency. After considering implementation, I realized the binaural calculation is more accurate on the React side, using state rather than audio buffer analysis. This will make the display more precise. I've been adding the logic to display this beat frequency in the header. The code is being refined, particularly `types.ts` for updated properties, and `useAudio.ts`, integrating `panner.setPosition` correctly. I'm working on `showOnMaster`, making sure panning and color work and don't introduce unintended combinations.


**Finalizing Feature Integration**

My latest focus has been on finalizing the architectural integration and ensuring existing functionality is preserved. I'm prioritizing the React-side calculations for the binaural beats to maintain accuracy. I've been reworking components like `Visualizer.tsx` to handle the new `sources` structure, updating `useAudio.ts` to manage 3D positioning with `PannerNode`, and modifying `OscillatorControls.tsx` with the new XYZ sliders and the "Show on Master" controls. Next, I'll update `Generator.tsx` to display binaural beat frequencies and properly route the audio analysers to the visualizer.


**Displaying Binaural Frequencies**

I'm now focusing on displaying the binaural beat frequency, and the design now features a dedicated section on the display for the calculated beat. Additionally, I'm working on refining the 3D controls for spatial positioning, and updating the Visualizer to work with multiple analyser nodes. The goal is a clean integration that respects the spatial audio controls and enhances user interaction. To streamline this process, I plan to combine the Binaural Display with the new Spatial Audio controls. This will lead to an upgrade in the UI to match the added features.

Aquí tienes la actualización que implementa **Audio Espacial 3D**, **Detección de Batidos Binaurales** y **Visualización Multicapa**.

### Resumen de Cambios:

1.  **Audio Espacial (3D):**
    *   Se ha sustituido el paneo estéreo simple por un nodo `PannerNode` completo.
    *   Ahora puedes controlar **X** (Izquierda/Derecha), **Z** (Adelante/Atrás) e **Y** (Arriba/Abajo).
    *   Se utiliza el modelo **HRTF** (Head-Related Transfer Function) para simular cómo escucha el oído humano en 3D real.

2.  **Detección Binaural:**
    *   En la sección "Resonancia Maestra", el sistema calcula matemáticamente la diferencia entre las dos frecuencias más prominentes activas y muestra el **Batido Binaural Resultante** (ej. "10 Hz (Alfa)").

3.  **Visualización Avanzada (Combinar/Separar):**
    *   El osciloscopio maestro ahora soporta **múltiples capas**.
    *   Cada oscilador tiene una opción **"Ver en Maestra"**.
    *   Si se activa, esa onda se dibuja encima de la mezcla principal con su propio **Color Personalizable**.
    *   La opción "Mezclar Audio" sigue activa por defecto, pero ahora puedes elegir visualmente si ver la suma o los componentes individuales.

payload.text:
**Resolving the Error**

I'm currently focused on the `App.tsx` file, specifically line 38. There's a persistent type error related to an unknown property, 'pan,' within the `OscillatorState`. The compiler is suggesting 'panX' instead. I'm investigating the `types` file to understand the expected properties of `OscillatorState` and identify the root cause of the mismatch. I'm aiming to find the right correction for the erroneous key.


**Analyzing the Type Definition**

I've been examining the `types.ts` file more closely. I can see that `OscillatorState` expects `panX`, `panY`, and `panZ` for spatial audio, and not just a single `pan` property. The compiler is correctly pointing out the discrepancy. It looks like I need to adjust the way I'm adding oscillators in `App.tsx` to include separate `panX`, `panY`, and `panZ` values when adding oscillators, likely setting the other pan values to 0.


**Implementing the Change**

I've replaced `pan: 0` with `panX: 0`. Now, I'm checking if `panY` and `panZ` require explicit values, even if they default to 0 in `useAudio.ts` hook. After a quick look, the `addOscillator` function handles default values for `panY` and `panZ`, so I'll trust those values. My focus now is on testing this updated implementation.


**Verifying the Solution**

I've updated `App.tsx` and the type error is gone. I replaced the single `pan` property with `panX` and removed `panY` and `panZ` because they default to 0. Now I'm testing the audio functionality to make sure that the stereo panning still functions correctly after this change. I'm focusing on confirming the expected audio output after the fix.


**Adjusting Frequency Parsing**

I'm now revising my audio engine setup. I'm focusing on the `handleAddToPlayer` function, which adds frequency items to the player. I noticed the current logic is too brittle and assumes items have a `numericalHz` value. My goal is to add better frequency parsing to handle cases where there is not a `numericalHz`, or it is a complex string that requires more complex parsing to extract the frequency data. I am adding a fallback mechanism to extract the first number from the `hz` string.


**Parsing Input Frequencies**

I'm now zeroing in on frequency parsing within the `handleAddToPlayer` function to handle diverse input formats. The existing approach assumed a `numericalHz` property, which is brittle. I'm building a more robust parsing mechanism to extract frequencies from various input strings, even if `numericalHz` is unavailable. payload.text: Los límites del selector de audio espacial de cada frecuencia deben ser que cada extremo sea el máximo posible contrario al otro extremo; o sea, por ejemplo, si selecciono el punto de x hasta la derecha, que sea completamente a la derecha y deje de oscilar por completo en la izquierda.

**Examining Audio Controls**

I'm currently focused on the spatial audio controls and their behaviour at extreme values. The central goal is to ensure a fully silent opposite channel when the control is pushed to its maximum value in either direction. This change aims for clear and predictable audio output.
